data.title: Franklin Templeton's U.S. government money fund has reached $1.98 billion in assets under management.
data.description: Franklin Templeton's official announcement revealed that its Franklin OnChain U.S. Government Money Fund (FOBXX) has been online for five years. The fund operates in the form of BENJI tokens and, as of April 2026, has become the second largest real-world asset (RWA) on the Stellar network, with assets under management reaching $1.98 billion.

data.title: Solana Launches Research Institute in Switzerland to Promote Blockchain Adoption in Europe
data.description: Solana has announced the establishment of the Solana Research Institute in Switzerland, aimed at assisting companies in evaluating and adopting public blockchains across Europe. According to NS3.AI, the institute will concentrate on understanding and navigating regulatory frameworks, including Europe's Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulation and a U.S. stablecoin bill known as GENIUS. This initiative is part of Solana's broader strategy to enhance blockchain integration and compliance within the European market.

data.title: CoinDesk 20 Index Rises as Aptos Leads Gains
data.description: The CoinDesk 20 index experienced an increase, trading at 2062.95, marking a 1.1% rise or 22.36 points since 4 p.m. ET on Wednesday. According to NS3.AI, nineteen of the index's twenty assets showed positive movement, with Aptos leading the gains at 4.4%.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin encountered resistance at a key level; continuous outflows from ETFs and increased market uncertainty due to disagreements within the Federal Reserve led to a wait-and-see approach.
data.description: Bitcoin traded sideways around $76,000 on Thursday, with market focus quickly shifting to internal policy disagreements and macroeconomic uncertainty after the Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged. Analysts pointed out that Bitcoin remains suppressed below the key resistance zone of $78,000 to $79,000, lacking short-term breakout momentum. Kraken's chief economist, Thomas Perfumo, stated that the market is currently more focused on the policy uncertainty caused by the Fed's internal "division" than on the inaction itself, especially given the coexistence of Chairman Jerome Powell's continued tenure and the potential succession of Kevin Warsh, resulting in a lack of clear policy transition. Glassnode data shows that Bitcoin remains "trapped" below the True Market Mean, with resistance concentrated in the $78,000 to $79,000 range and support between $65,000 and $70,000. Although selling pressure has eased somewhat, demand is insufficient to support a sustained upward breakout. On the macroeconomic front, the Fed's rare and significant disagreement has been interpreted by the market as increased uncertainty regarding the inflation path. Analysts from Bitget Wallet and 21Shares, among others, point out that expectations of "maintaining high interest rates for a longer period" are suppressing the performance of risk assets, and the crypto market has entered a wait-and-see phase. In terms of fund flows, US Bitcoin spot ETFs have recorded net outflows for three consecutive days, with a single-day outflow of approximately $138 million on April 29th; Ethereum ETFs saw outflows of approximately $87.7 million during the same period. Although some individual products still see inflows, the overall trend indicates that institutional demand is cooling. Meanwhile, while CME holdings and ETF asset management scale are stabilizing, there are no strong signs of a return to the market. The derivatives market shows that short positions in perpetual contracts have reached historical highs. Improved sentiment could trigger a squeeze, but the current market remains dominated by a low-volatility, low-confidence consolidation structure. Overall, Bitcoin is caught in a tug-of-war between improving support structures and weak demand, with continued ETF outflows, policy uncertainty, and macroeconomic risks collectively suppressing its breakthrough of key resistance levels. (The Block)

data.title: TRM Labs: 76% of global cryptocurrency hacks in the first four months of 2026 were linked to North Korean hackers.
data.description: A recent report from blockchain intelligence firm TRM Labs reveals that approximately 76% of global cryptocurrency hacking losses in the first four months of 2026 were linked to North Korean-related organizations, with a total stolen amount of approximately $577 million. North Korea's share of global cryptocurrency theft continues to rise: 22% in 2022, 37% in 2023, 39% in 2024, rising to 64% in 2025, and further increasing to 76% in 2026, with cumulative illegal profits exceeding $6 billion since 2017. The report points out that these losses primarily stemmed from two major incidents in April: the $292 million attack on KelpDAO and the $285 million theft from Drift Protocol, these two attacks combined accounting for approximately 3% of all hacking incidents during the same period. (The Block)

data.title: Polymarket Collaborates with Chainalysis to Enhance Platform Regulation
data.description:
Polymarket has announced a partnership with blockchain analytics firm Chainalysis Inc. to improve the regulation of its platform. According to Foresight News, Chainalysis has developed tools for Polymarket's cryptocurrency exchange, including a detection model designed to identify patterns consistent with insider information within prediction markets. Additional tools will provide evidence for law enforcement and regulatory bodies, while also enhancing protection against cybersecurity threats.

In recent months, the industry has faced significant pressure to combat insider trading due to a series of well-timed and profitable cases. Neal Kumar, Polymarket's Chief Legal Officer, stated that the collaboration with Chainalysis will assist in identifying problematic trades. Last month, Polymarket updated its rules to prohibit trading based on stolen confidential information and illegal insider knowledge, as well as betting by individuals who can influence event outcomes.

data.title: Polymarket launches on-chain market integrity monitoring system to combat insider trading.
data.description: Polymarket announced the launch of an on-chain market integrity monitoring system solution to monitor trading activity and enforce platform market compliance rules. Built in partnership with blockchain data analytics company Chainalysis, the system covers Polymarket's entire DeFi trading process, including real-time on-chain analysis of trading, holding, and settlement data. It uses multi-layered monitoring mechanisms to identify potential violations, with a focus on insider trading and market manipulation. Polymarket stated that all its transactions are completed on a public blockchain, and this partnership will further amplify the advantages of on-chain transparency, enabling regulators and law enforcement to obtain verifiable on-chain evidence, thereby establishing new compliance standards in the prediction market field. Polymarket founder and CEO Shayne Coplan stated that the platform has emphasized transparency and traceability from its inception, and this partnership will further strengthen its position as a "trusted source of market information." (Businesswire)

data.title: Carmignac Economist Discusses ECB's Hawkish Stance Amid Economic Concerns
data.description: Carmignac economist Apolline Menut has expressed that the European Central Bank (ECB) has demonstrated a more hawkish response function. According to Jin10, Menut noted that even during communications following the Iran war, around the March meeting, the ECB had already undertaken significant work by providing a clear framework through scenario analysis. Each scenario implied a certain degree of interest rate hikes, depending on oil and gas prices. Given the latest developments in oil prices, Menut suggests that the current situation might be between the baseline and adverse scenarios. From the data, it appears that stagflation has returned, as both soft and hard data are not very optimistic.

data.title: ECB President Lagarde: Hard Data Largely Meets Expectations
data.description: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde stated that recent hard data aligns closely with expectations. According to Jin10, Lagarde emphasized the importance of monitoring economic indicators to ensure that they continue to reflect anticipated trends. Her comments come amid ongoing assessments of the eurozone's economic performance and the ECB's policy measures.

data.title: ECB President Lagarde Discusses Potential Rate Hike Options
data.description: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde stated that various options, including the possibility of raising interest rates, were discussed. According to Jin10, the discussions are part of the ECB's ongoing efforts to address economic challenges and ensure financial stability in the region.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Bank of America Maintains Gold Price Forecast at $6,000 for Next 12 Months
data.description: Bank of America has reiterated its forecast for gold prices, predicting they will reach $6,000 within the next 12 months. According to Jin10, this projection reflects the bank's continued confidence in the precious metal's potential for significant appreciation. The forecast comes amid ongoing global economic uncertainties and fluctuating market conditions, which have historically influenced gold prices. Bank of America's outlook suggests that investors may see substantial gains in the gold market over the coming year.

data.title: Gemini obtains CFTC derivatives clearing license, advancing the construction of a "full-stack CFTC compliance system".
data.description: Gemini's Olympus division has officially received a Derivatives Clearing Organization (DCO) license from the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), authorizing it to independently handle settlement, risk management, and collateral management as an internal clearing entity. Gemini co-founder Cameron Winklevoss stated that this approval signifies the platform's expansion from the spot market to a "full-stack trading system" encompassing prediction markets, futures, options, and perpetual contracts. Previously, Gemini obtained a Designated Contract Market (DCM) license in December 2025 and plans to integrate its prediction market and derivatives businesses into its Titan platform. (The Block)

data.title: ECB President Lagarde: Core Inflation Shows Little Change in Recent Months
data.description: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde stated that core inflation has shown little change over the past few months. According to Jin10, Lagarde's comments come amid ongoing discussions about the economic outlook and monetary policy in the eurozone. The ECB continues to monitor inflation trends closely as it assesses the need for potential policy adjustments.

data.title: Trump plans to sign an executive order to expand retirement savings coverage.
data.description: An anonymous White House official revealed that Trump is about to sign an executive order expanding coverage of savings plans for workers not receiving employer-sponsored retirement benefits. This move signifies a return of the US government's policy focus to livelihood and economic issues. The official revealed these details before the official announcement. It's noteworthy that the White House had previously approved a proposal to include cryptocurrency in 401(k) retirement plans, creating a policy synergy with this new policy – ​​both broadening retirement security channels for ordinary workers and paving the way for digital assets to enter the mainstream retirement system. Further developments warrant continued monitoring. (Bloomberg)

data.title: ECB President Lagarde: Inflation Risks Tilt Upward
data.description: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde has indicated that inflation risks are skewed to the upside. According to Jin10, Lagarde's comments come amid ongoing concerns about economic stability and inflationary pressures within the Eurozone. The ECB continues to monitor these developments closely as they assess potential impacts on monetary policy and economic growth.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Russia's Central Bank Reserves Decline Slightly
data.description: Russia's central bank reported a decrease in its gold and foreign exchange reserves for the week ending April 24, 2026. According to Jin10, the reserves totaled $771.7 billion, down from the previous value of $779.5 billion. This slight decline reflects ongoing adjustments in Russia's financial strategy.

data.title: European STOXX 600 Index Reaches Intraday High with 1% Increase
data.description: The European STOXX 600 index has seen its gains expand to 1%, marking a new intraday high. According to ChainCatcher, this development highlights the positive momentum in European markets. The index's performance reflects investor optimism and market strength across various sectors.

data.title: U.S. Unemployment Claims Fall Below Expectations
data.description: The number of Americans continuing to claim unemployment benefits for the week ending April 18 was reported at 1.785 million, according to ChainCatcher. This figure is lower than the market expectation of 1.815 million. The previous week's data was revised from 1.821 million to 1.808 million, indicating a slight improvement in the labor market.

data.title: Tether Gold Treasury Firm Aurelion Reports Q2 Financials, Plans Digital Transition
data.description:
Aurelion, a Nasdaq-listed Tether Gold treasury company, has released its financial report for the second quarter of fiscal year 2026. According to Odaily, the company is accelerating its transition to a digital gold treasury asset firm. As of March 31, 2026, Aurelion holds 33,318 XAUT, with a net asset value of $116.4 million, translating to a NAV per share of $3.16.

Additionally, Aurelion has committed to investing 10,000 XAUT, valued at approximately $48 million, into the XAUE protocol. This move aims to deploy tokenized gold assets to maintain underlying gold exposure while seeking potential returns.

data.title: U.S. Economy Grows in Q1 Amid Rising Government Spending
data.description: The U.S. economy experienced growth in the first quarter, driven by increased government spending, according to Jin10. However, this growth may be temporary as the ongoing conflict with Iran has led to higher gasoline prices, further straining household budgets. Data released by the U.S. Department of Commerce's Bureau of Economic Analysis on Thursday showed that the preliminary annualized quarter-on-quarter growth rate of the U.S. real GDP was 2.0% in Q1, up from 0.5% in the fourth quarter of 2025. The surge in investment in the artificial intelligence sector and the construction of data centers supporting this technology continued to boost corporate spending on equipment. Nonetheless, consumer spending, a key driver of the economy, has slowed further, with its momentum weakening even before the U.S.-Iran conflict.
